title: btrfs driver + graphdriver no Data inspect for link to subvolume
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Using `podman inspect {container}` the GraphDriver section does not carry a link to the topmost subvolume used (overlay driver shows the lower+upper+work+merge directory)

```
          "GraphDriver": {
               "Name": "btrfs",
               "Data": null
          },
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Enable btrfs as storage + graph driver

2. `podman inspect {container}`


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The data is present in runc json as well as userdata, so seems its just not exposed in the print out.
```
/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/btrfs-containers/7b0b90576a82f97b3300d431a0433d2cc8bdbc46937149848c5e7c1c05e18813/userdata/config.json

/run/user/1000/runc/7b0b90576a82f97b3300d431a0433d2cc8bdbc46937149848c5e7c1c05e18813/state.json

"rootfs":"/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/btrfs/subvolumes/d8e035f06e4fb25325fa3a460454b6b8bcdbfc0456fe27fee991584262589867"
```

**Output of `podman version`:**
```

Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.19
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@vans163 Interested in opening a PR. Not too many people work with BTRFS driver.  Sorry that this issue got lost in the flood of issues.

> @vans163 Interested in opening a PR. Not too many people work with BTRFS driver. Sorry that this issue got lost in the flood of issues.

Actually we stopped using the BTRFS driver and instead use the kernel overlay driver (with the underlying fs being BTFRS).
